name: Fire TV Stick (all generations)
notes: Connect Fire TV via USB (micro-USB or USB-C depending on generation). Enable Developer Options: Settings > My Fire TV > About > click "Build Number" 7 times. Then enable ADB debugging. Fire TV must be on the same network or connected via USB with ADB over TCP.

safe.name: Safe debloat
safe.desc: Remove ads and telemetry without breaking core features.

moderate.name: Moderate debloat
moderate.desc: Remove Amazon apps and services you don't use. Keeps Alexa.

aggressive.name: Full debloat
aggressive.desc: Strip Amazon ecosystem. Install a custom launcher afterward.

recommended_apps.name: Wolf Launcher
recommended_apps.desc: Minimal, ad-free launcher for Fire TV. Replaces the Amazon home screen.

recommended_apps.name: SmartTubeNext
recommended_apps.desc: Ad-free YouTube client for Android TV.

name: RG35XX / RG35XX Plus / RG35XX H / RG35XX SP

flash_steps.name: Write image to SD card
flash_steps.command: dd if={image} of={sd_device} bs=4M status=progress conv=fsync
flash_steps.description: Write the CFW image to a microSD card (TF1 slot)

flash_steps.name: Add game ROMs
flash_steps.description: Copy your ROM files to the ROMS partition on the SD card. Organize by system folder (GBA, SNES, PS1, etc.).

notes: Allwinner H700 handheld. Uses two SD card slots: TF1 for OS, TF2 for extra storage. Flash CFW to TF1. Format SD as FAT32 (for cards under 64GB) or exFAT (64GB+). First boot may take a few minutes while the OS expands the partition.

name: BQ Aquaris U Plus

flash_steps.name: Download firmware
flash_steps.description: Download the selected firmware image

flash_steps.name: Verify integrity
flash_steps.description: Check SHA256 against the firmware registry

flash_steps.name: Unlock bootloader
flash_steps.description: Unlock the bootloader (erases all data). BQ required requesting an unlock code via their developer portal, which is now defunct. If the bootloader is already unlocked, skip this step. If locked, search XDA forums for community unlock methods.

flash_steps.name: Flash TWRP recovery
flash_steps.description: Flash TWRP custom recovery via fastboot

flash_steps.name: Flash firmware
flash_steps.description: Flash firmware via fastboot

flash_steps.name: Post-flash setup
flash_steps.description: Boot into recovery, wipe cache/dalvik, then reboot to verify installation

notes: Snapdragon 430 (MSM8937) phone from BQ (2016). BQ ceased operations in 2020, so no official firmware updates are available. Community ROMs (LineageOS 14.1, /e/OS) are the primary options. Bootloader unlock originally required a code from BQ's developer portal (now offline) — check XDA for alternatives if locked. The silver color variant has identical internals to all other color options.

name: Pixel 7

flash_steps.name: Download firmware
flash_steps.description: Download the selected firmware image

flash_steps.name: Verify integrity
flash_steps.description: Check SHA256 against the firmware registry

flash_steps.name: Unlock bootloader
flash_steps.description: Unlock the bootloader (erases all data)

flash_steps.name: Flash firmware
flash_steps.description: Flash firmware via fastboot

flash_steps.name: Relock bootloader
flash_steps.description: Relock bootloader for security (optional, only for stock/GrapheneOS)

flash_steps.name: Post-flash setup
flash_steps.description: Boot and verify installation

notes: Tensor G2 phone. OEM unlocking must be enabled in Developer Settings first.

name: Kobo E-Reader (all models)

flash_steps.name: Connect Kobo via USB
flash_steps.description: Plug in your Kobo via USB. When prompted on the device, choose "Connect" (not "Charge only") to mount as USB storage.

flash_steps.name: Install KOReader
flash_steps.description: Download the KOReader Kobo release ZIP and extract it to the root of the Kobo's mounted filesystem. KOReader appears as a menu option in Nickel (the stock OS).

flash_steps.name: Install NickelMenu (optional)
flash_steps.description: NickelMenu adds custom entries to the Kobo home screen, making it easy to launch KOReader or Plato from the stock UI.

flash_steps.name: Safely eject and disconnect
flash_steps.description: Safely eject the Kobo from your file manager, then unplug USB.

notes: KOReader install is non-destructive — it runs alongside the stock Nickel OS. No jailbreak or exploit needed. Works on all Kobo models (Clara, Libra, Sage, Elipsa, Nia, Forma, Aura, etc.). To uninstall, simply delete the .adds/koreader folder from the Kobo's storage.

name: ThinkPad X200

flash_steps.name: Access SPI chip
flash_steps.description: Remove the keyboard and palmrest to access the SOIC-8 SPI flash chip. Attach a Pomona 5250 clip or use a CH341A programmer with clip leads.

flash_steps.name: Backup original firmware
flash_steps.description: Read the full 8MB chip contents

flash_steps.name: Flash Libreboot
flash_steps.description: Write the Libreboot image to the full chip

notes: Intel GM45 ThinkPad. Fully supported by Libreboot (no binary blobs). First flash requires external SPI programmer (CH341A ~$5) with Pomona 5250 clip. Intel ME is neutralized by Libreboot's build system. After Libreboot is installed, internal flashing works.

name: ThinkPad X230

flash_steps.name: Backup original firmware
flash_steps.description: Read and save the current firmware. CRITICAL — keep this file safe.

flash_steps.name: Flash Coreboot
flash_steps.description: Write the Coreboot image to the BIOS region only. The -i bios flag flashes only the BIOS region, preserving the Intel ME and GbE regions.

post_flash.name: Reboot into Coreboot
post_flash.description: Power cycle the machine (full shutdown, not restart)

notes: Ivy Bridge ThinkPad. Dual SPI chip layout (4MB upper + 8MB lower). For first flash from vendor BIOS: use 1vyrain exploit (no hardware needed) or SPI clip with CH341A programmer. After Coreboot is installed, subsequent updates can use flashrom -p internal. Skulls project provides pre-built images with SeaBIOS payload.

name: Miyoo Mini / Miyoo Mini Plus

flash_steps.name: Copy to FAT32 SD card
flash_steps.description: Format a microSD card as FAT32. Extract the CFW ZIP directly to the root of the SD card. Insert and boot.

notes: ARM926 handheld. Unlike most devices, Miyoo Mini CFW is NOT a raw disk image — it's a ZIP extracted onto a FAT32 SD card. Format the card as FAT32, extract the Onion OS ZIP to the root, then insert and boot. First boot takes 1-2 minutes.

flash_steps.name: Download firmware
flash_steps.description: Download the selected firmware image

flash_steps.name: Unlock bootloader
flash_steps.description: Unlock the bootloader (erases all data)

flash_steps.name: Flash firmware
flash_steps.description: Flash firmware via fastboot

flash_steps.name: Post-flash setup
flash_steps.description: Boot and verify installation

notes: MediaTek Dimensity 7200 Pro phone. Glyph LED interface only works on Nothing OS. Bootloader unlock via standard fastboot process.

name: Nothing Phone (1)

flash_steps.name: Download firmware

name: PinePhone

flash_steps.name: Write image to SD card
flash_steps.command: dd if={image} of={sd_device} bs=4M status=progress conv=fsync
flash_steps.description: Write the OS image to a microSD card

flash_steps.name: Insert SD card and boot
flash_steps.description: Insert the microSD card into the PinePhone. The device boots from SD card by default (SD takes priority over eMMC).

flash_steps.name: Install to eMMC (optional)
flash_steps.description: Once booted from SD, use the OS installer to write the image to internal eMMC for better performance. Or use Jumpdrive to expose eMMC as USB mass storage from a PC.

post_flash.name: First boot setup
post_flash.description: Configure WiFi, user account, and PIN on first boot

notes: Allwinner A64 phone. Boot priority: SD card > eMMC. To flash eMMC directly, use Jumpdrive (https://github.com/dreemurrs-embedded/Jumpdrive) to expose eMMC as USB mass storage. Tow-Boot bootloader recommended for multi-boot and USB boot support.

name: PinePhone Pro

flash_steps.name: Write image to SD card
flash_steps.command: dd if={image} of={sd_device} bs=4M status=progress conv=fsync

notes: Rockchip RK3399 phone. Same form factor as PinePhone. Tow-Boot bootloader strongly recommended — enables USB boot and multi-boot selection. Use Jumpdrive for direct eMMC access from a PC.

name: PineTab 2

flash_steps.name: Write image to SD card
flash_steps.command: dd if={image} of={sd_device} bs=4M status=progress conv=fsync

notes: Rockchip RK3566 tablet. Boots from SD card by default. Detachable keyboard available. Lower power consumption than PinePhone Pro.

name: Librem 5

flash_steps.name: Write image to SD card
flash_steps.command: dd if={image} of={sd_device} bs=4M status=progress conv=fsync

flash_steps.name: Insert SD card and boot
flash_steps.description: Insert the SD card. Hold Volume Down during boot to enter the boot menu and select SD card.

flash_steps.name: Install to eMMC (optional)
flash_steps.description: Use uuu (Universal Update Utility) for direct eMMC flashing via USB, or install from the running SD-booted OS.

notes: NXP i.MX 8M Quad phone. Hardware kill switches for WiFi/BT, cellular, and camera/mic. Separate M.2 cards for wireless components. Default OS is PureOS (Phosh). For direct eMMC flashing, use Purism's uuu-based flashing scripts.

name: Raspberry Pi 3 Model B

flash_steps.name: Insert your SD card
flash_steps.description: Insert a microSD card (8GB minimum, 32GB recommended) into your computer's SD card reader. If using a USB adapter, plug it in now.

flash_steps.name: Select an OS image
flash_steps.description: Choose which operating system to install from the list above. Raspberry Pi OS is recommended for most users.

flash_steps.name: Flash the image
flash_steps.command: dd if={image} of={sd_device} bs=4M status=progress conv=fsync
flash_steps.description: OSmosis will download the image, verify its checksum, decompress it, and write it to the SD card. This may take several minutes.

flash_steps.name: Configure (optional)
flash_steps.description: After flashing, OSmosis can pre-configure WiFi credentials, enable SSH, and set a hostname on the boot partition — so the Pi boots ready to use headlessly.

flash_steps.name: Insert SD card into Pi
flash_steps.description: Remove the SD card from your computer and insert it into the Pi 3B's microSD slot on the underside of the board.

flash_steps.name: Power on
flash_steps.description: Connect a 5V/2.5A micro-USB power supply to the Pi. The green ACT LED will flicker as it boots. First boot may take 1-3 minutes.

notes: The Pi 3B v1.2 has a BCM2837 (quad-core Cortex-A53 @ 1.2GHz). Boots from SD card by default. USB boot requires a one-time OTP bit set via raspi-config or config.txt (program_usb_boot_mode=1).

name: Galaxy Tab S 10.5 LTE

flash_steps.name: Download firmware
flash_steps.description: Download the selected firmware image

flash_steps.name: Verify integrity
flash_steps.description: Check SHA256 against the firmware registry

flash_steps.name: Backup current firmware
flash_steps.description: Back up current partitions before flashing

flash_steps.name: Flash recovery
flash_steps.description: Flash custom recovery (TWRP)

flash_steps.name: Flash ROM via sideload
flash_steps.description: Sideload the custom ROM via ADB

flash_steps.name: Post-flash setup
flash_steps.description: Reboot and verify installation

notes: Exynos 5420 tablet. Ensure Download Mode before flashing (Power + Home + VolDown).

name: Galaxy Ace 2

flash_steps.name: Download firmware
flash_steps.description: Download or build the selected firmware image

flash_steps.name: Verify integrity
flash_steps.description: Check SHA256 hash of downloaded image

flash_steps.name: Backup current firmware
flash_steps.description: Back up current partitions via ADB before flashing

flash_steps.name: Flash U-Boot bootloader
flash_steps.description: Flash U-Boot stemmy to the boot partition. U-Boot replaces Samsung's secondary bootloader and provides fastboot mode for loading the mainline Linux kernel. This is the first step for any pmOS install.

flash_steps.name: Flash rootfs to USERDATA
flash_steps.description: Flash the postmarketOS rootfs image to the USERDATA partition. On the Ace 2, USERDATA is the largest partition (~2GB) and is used as the root filesystem for pmOS.

flash_steps.name: Flash kernel
flash_steps.description: Flash the pmOS boot image (kernel + initramfs + DTB) to the RECOVERY partition. U-Boot loads the kernel from this partition.

flash_steps.name: Reboot into Bender
flash_steps.description: Remove USB cable, then hold Volume Up + Home + Power for 3 seconds to boot. U-Boot will load the mainline kernel from RECOVERY, which mounts rootfs from USERDATA.

post_flash.name: Connect to WiFi
post_flash.description: On first boot, connect via USB networking (SSH to 172.16.42.1) and configure WiFi with connmanctl. Once connected, Bender's web UI is available at http://bender.local:8080 or the device's IP.

post_flash.name: Set up API key
post_flash.description: Open http://bender.local:8080 in a browser (or on the device screen if display patches are applied). Paste your Anthropic API key or scan a QR code. Bender will introduce itself and ask what you want to use the device for.

notes:
NovaThor U8500 phone with 768MB RAM and 4GB storage. Tight but viable for Bender — the agent server runs locally, inference is cloud-side.
postmarketOS support is at "testing" tier. OSmosis provides kernel patches for display (MCDE DPI + WS2401 panel) and audio (MSP I2S + AB8500 codec) that are not yet upstream.
Boot chain: Samsung PBL → U-Boot stemmy → mainline kernel (6.x) CAVEAT: U-Boot path failed on our test unit (SBL ignores Kernel PIT partition). Android app route is the current viable path.
Enter Download Mode: Volume Down + Home + Power. Two panel variants exist (LMS380KF01 90%, S6D27A1 10%) — U-Boot auto-detects via Samsung's lcdtype ATAG.
GPS chip: Broadcom BCM4751 (broken in pmOS). NFC: PNX544, only on GT-I8160P variant (antenna in back plate).

variants.notes: Has NFC (PNX544 chip, antenna in back plate)

known_issues.description: If the battery fully depletes, the device cannot charge because it cannot boot to the kernel, and it cannot boot if battery is too low (shuts down before reaching kernel). This is also the case on Android. Recovery requires an external battery charger or a replacement battery.
Bluetooth has no device address set after boot. Must manually configure: apk add bluez-btmgmt && btmgmt public-addr <bdaddr>
Phosh may have wrong DSI scaling, making the lockscreen unusable. Fix: set scale = 1 in /usr/share/phosh/phoc.ini under [output:DSI-1].
USB networking only works if USB is plugged in while "postmarketOS Loading..." is shown. Timing-sensitive.
All Codina variants use PRCMU firmware "U8420" that caps CPU to ~800MHz instead of the ~1GHz the SoC supports. Likely a pure software restriction.

Real-world testing on one GT-I8160 unit showed the SBL ignores the Kernel PIT partition entirely — flashing U-Boot, mainline zImage, or garbage to it has zero effect. SBL partition is read-only (Heimdall rejects writes). Secure boot fuses are NOT blown; the issue is partition mapping. The pmOS wiki U-Boot path may work on other units but failed on ours. Android app route is the viable alternative.

sensors: Amstaos TMD2672 light and proximity sensor

name: Galaxy Note II

flash_steps.name: Flash Replicant Recovery
flash_steps.description: Flash Replicant's own recovery (required for Replicant ROMs)

flash_steps.name: Flash ROM via sideload
flash_steps.description: Sideload Replicant via ADB in Replicant Recovery

notes: Exynos 4412 phone. Replicant requires its own recovery (not TWRP). Stock recovery will reject unsigned ZIPs. Enter Download Mode with Volume Down + Home + Power.

name: TrimUI Smart Pro

flash_steps.name: Write image to SD card
flash_steps.command: dd if={image} of={sd_device} bs=4M status=progress conv=fsync

notes: Allwinner T113 handheld with 4.96" IPS screen. Write CFW image to microSD card. KNULLI is the primary recommended CFW.
